name: Establishment of West Pakistan Act 1955 (One Unit)
era: Dominion of Pakistan — the Interim Framework (1947–1956)
key_provisions: One province for the entire western wing; East Bengal became East Pakistan
Parity principle: equal representation for the two wings regardless of population
Second Constituent Assembly (convened summer 1955) enacted the scheme
demise: One Unit was dissolved by Yahya Khan's regime with effect from 1 July 1970, restoring the four provinces.
